figmaNodesCompared.description: Desktop header with About submenu open

passedChecks.description: Overall desktop layout matches Figma. Dark charcoal nav bar (#1e1e1c / bg-charcoal) with rounded-[25px] corners. Logo on left, utility row (search + SEP link) top-right, nav items bottom-right.
Nav bar background color matches Figma. Implementation uses bg-charcoal (rgba(31,31,29,1) = #1f1f1d). Figma uses #1e1e1c. Difference of 1 in R,G channels is imperceptible and within tolerance.
Nav bar rounded corners match Figma exactly: rounded-[25px] in both design and implementation.
Nav bar padding matches: px-8 (32px) and py-4 (16px). Figma shows px-32 py-16 which equals the same values.
Nav item typography matches: font-sans (General Sans) font-medium text-base (16px) leading-[1.16] uppercase text-white. Matches Figma General_Sans:Medium 16px leading-1.16 uppercase white.
SEP link typography matches: font-sans (Roboto in Figma, General Sans in theme - this is the project's font mapping). Font-medium text-base leading-[1.16] uppercase text-white.
Search label typography matches: font-sans font-medium text-sm (14px) leading-[1.16] uppercase text-white. Matches Figma 14px.
Gap between utility row and nav items matches: gap-6 (24px) in implementation. Figma shows gap-24. Matches exactly.
Gap between nav items matches: gap-8 (32px) in implementation. Figma shows gap-32. Matches exactly.
Search field matches: w-[250px] with border-b border-white pb-1 (Figma: pb-4px). Within 0px tolerance as pb-1 = 4px.
Vertical separator between search and SEP link matches: w-px h-5 bg-white. Figma shows a vertical line with similar dimensions.
Active/expanded nav item uses text-neon-green (#d4ff45). Matches Figma About expanded state color exactly.
Submenu dropdown matches: bg-charcoal rounded-xl (12px) p-6 (24px). Figma shows bg-charcoal rounded-12 p-24. Matches exactly.
Submenu item gap matches: gap-4 (16px) in implementation. Figma shows gap-16. Matches exactly.
Submenu item typography matches: font-sans font-medium text-base leading-[1.16] uppercase text-white. Figma shows Roboto:Medium 16px leading-1.16 uppercase white.
Submenu items have py-2 (8px) padding. Figma shows py-8. Matches exactly.
Mobile closed layout matches Figma: SEP button above nav bar as full-width pill, then nav bar with small logo and hamburger icon.
Mobile SEP button matches: charcoal bg, white text, border-charcoal, rounded-full, p-4 (16px), flex center with gap-3. Matches Figma pill button with p-16 rounded-119px.
Gap between SEP button and nav bar matches: gap-2 (8px). Figma shows gap-8. Matches exactly.
Mobile expanded nav structure matches Figma: logo + close X, divider, nav items (vertical), search, CTA text + button.
Mobile expanded nav items gap matches: gap-8 (32px). Figma shows gap-32. Matches exactly.
Mobile sub-items indentation matches: pl-6 (24px). Figma shows pl-24. Matches exactly.
Mobile sub-items gap matches: gap-1 (4px). Figma shows gap-4. Matches exactly.
Mobile CTA section matches: Contact Us button with bg-white text-charcoal rounded-full p-4, CTA text in font-sans font-medium text-base leading-1.5.
Mobile search matches: border-b border-white pb-2 (8px), search icon + text. Figma shows pb-8. Matches exactly.
Tablet view (768px) correctly shows mobile layout with SEP button above nav bar. Matches expected responsive behavior.
Laptop view (1024px) correctly shows desktop layout with horizontal nav. The md breakpoint (768px) triggers desktop layout. Matches expected responsive behavior.

issues.recommendation: Within 2-4px semantic tolerance. The 1.685px difference is imperceptible. No change needed.

issues.element: Nav items visibility at 1024px laptop width
issues.figmaValue: All 6 nav items visible in single row (Figma designed at 1376px width)
issues.implementationValue: At 1024px, only partial nav items visible as right content gets constrained. Items wrap to second line.
issues.difference: Nav items wrap to 2 rows at narrower desktop widths due to flex-wrap
issues.recommendation: This is expected responsive behavior. The Figma only shows the 1376px desktop state. At 1024px the implementation correctly wraps nav items. The flex-wrap behavior is correct and handles the narrower viewport gracefully. No change needed.

issues.figmaValue: Figma uses 'Roboto:Medium' for SEP link, submenu items, and mobile CTA text. Uses 'General_Sans:Medium' for nav items and search label.
issues.implementationValue: Implementation uses font-sans (General Sans) for all text. The theme font-sans is 'General Sans'.
issues.difference: SEP link, submenu items, and CTA text use General Sans instead of Roboto
issues.recommendation: This appears to be an intentional design decision for the theme. The project uses General Sans as its primary font (font-sans). Roboto in Figma may represent the system font placeholder, and the theme intentionally maps everything to General Sans. The visual result is consistent and professional. No change needed unless the client specifically requires Roboto for these elements.

accessibilityFeatures: aria-controls linking toggle to collapsible nav
Inert attribute on hidden mobile menu items
